parameters.jsCode:
const trigger = $('get_file_id').first().json;
const post_id = trigger.post_id;
const topic = trigger.topic;
const description = trigger.description || '';
const research_drive_url = trigger.research_drive_url;
const folder_id = trigger.folder_id || '';

const binaryBuffer = await this.helpers.getBinaryDataBuffer(0, 'data');
const research = JSON.parse(binaryBuffer.toString('utf-8'));

const topResults = (research.results || []).slice(0, 15);
const allResults = (research.results || []);

const summary = topResults.map((r, i) =>
  `[${i + 1}] ${r.title}\nURL: ${r.url || 'N/A'}\nSource: ${r.source || 'web'}\n${(r.content || '').substring(0, 700)}`
).join('\n\n---\n\n');

const research_summary = allResults.slice(0, 20).map((r, i) =>
  `[${i + 1}] (${r.source || 'web'}) ${r.title}\nURL: ${r.url || 'N/A'}\n${(r.content || '').substring(0, 350)}`
).join('\n\n---\n\n');

const prompt_request = `You are a top 1% LinkedIn content strategist. Posts you write get screenshot, quote-tweeted, and re-shared by senior B2B operators, founders, and technical leaders. Reference voice: Packy McCormick, Lenny Rachitsky, Auren Hoffman, Stratechery briefings.

â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•
TOPIC LOCK (most important rule)
â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•
OFFICIAL TOPIC: ${topic}
DESCRIPTION: ${description || 'N/A'}

ENTITY EXTRACTION FROM DESCRIPTION (do this FIRST, paste the result into both briefs):
  Step 1: Read the DESCRIPTION above. Extract every named entity (tools, companies, products, frameworks, people, papers, reports).
  Step 2: List them as REQUIRED_ENTITIES_V1 and REQUIRED_ENTITIES_V2 - distribute them so V1 and V2 use DIFFERENT subsets (no overlap if possible). Each variation must use AT LEAST 2 of the listed entities by exact name in the body of the post.
  Step 3: If the description has fewer than 4 named entities total, instruct the writer to web-search and add 2 more SPECIFIC named entities (real companies, real tools, real teams) that fit the official topic - never generic phrases.
  Step 4: BAN_LIST: forbid the writer from leading the post with these over-used proof points unless they appear by name in the description: Klarna 2.3M, Intercom Fin, LMSYS Chatbot Arena, MMLU, Stanford HELM. If the description does not name them, they must NOT be the lead stat or central case.

EVERY brief you write must instruct the writer AI that the post must:
  - Stay tightly inside the OFFICIAL TOPIC. Do not drift to adjacent topics.
  - Use AT LEAST 2 of the REQUIRED_ENTITIES from the brief by exact name in the post body.
  - Treat the BAN_LIST as off-limits unless explicitly named in the DESCRIPTION.
  - Make the central claim of the post a direct argument about the OFFICIAL TOPIC.
  - If the writer's first draft does not have a central claim that paraphrases the topic, the brief should tell the writer to rewrite.

YOUR TASK
Read the topic + research dossier. Produce TWO complete, self-contained writing briefs. The downstream AI will use these briefs to write the FINAL post text.

THE TWO ANGLES MUST BE DEEPLY DIFFERENTIATED. Pick angles dynamically based on what the topic supports best (do not blindly copy):
  - contrarian / myth-buster        vs   builder playbook
  - data-driven analysis            vs   field story / single case study
  - "what most people miss"          vs   "what the winners actually do"
  - market shift / strategic         vs   operator how-to / tactical
  - macro pattern across companies   vs   one specific company in depth

NON-OVERLAP RULE (critical)
The two posts together must read like they came from two different sharp operators:
  - Each post must lead with a DIFFERENT named company / case study as primary proof.
  - Each post must cite a DIFFERENT primary statistic in the hook. No reusing the same number across the two.
  - Different cadence: if V1 uses bullets, V2 should be more prose-driven (or vice versa).
  - Different emotional tone: e.g. V1 = uncomfortable recognition, V2 = tactical confidence.

RESEARCH DOSSIER (extract real names, numbers, dates, URLs from here - do not invent):
${summary}

EVERY BRIEF MUST INSTRUCT THE WRITER AI TO:

1. RESEARCH FIRST - Use the Firecrawl web search tool for at least 3 fresh searches before writing. Prioritise: HBR, McKinsey, BCG, Gartner, Forrester, MIT Sloan, a16z, Stratechery, official Anthropic / OpenAI / Google / Meta research, Lenny's Newsletter, First Round Review, reputable journalism. Pull stats and named examples from 2024-2026.

2. WRITE IN 5 EXPLICIT BLOCKS, each separated by a blank line:

   BLOCK 1 - HOOK (1-2 lines, MAX 140 chars total)
     Pass the scroll test: the reader sees only these 2 lines in the feed and decides to expand.
     Allowed: bold contrarian claim, surprising specific stat, sharp pattern observation, before/after framing.
     BANNED: question openers, "I am excited", "AI is transforming everything", motivational opener, vague generalisation.

   BLOCK 2 - CONTEXT (2-4 short lines)
     Why this matters NOW. Anchor in a specific market shift, recent event, or named-source datapoint ("Per Gartner's 2025 report..."). Set the stakes.

   BLOCK 3 - INSIGHTS / BREAKDOWN (3-7 lines, may use bullets, numbered list, or short prose)
     The substance. Specific facts, named companies, real numbers, contrast pairs, a 2-3 step model.
     Every line must contain at least one CONCRETE noun (a company, a number, a tool, a tactic).

   BLOCK 4 - TAKEAWAY (its own standalone paragraph, 1-2 lines, blank line above and below)
     The single line a reader will screenshot. Must work standalone with no context.
     Bad: "AI is the future." Good: "Models are commodities. Context is the moat."

   BLOCK 5 - CTA + HASHTAGS
     CTA: one sharp specific question that triggers comments from operators. NEVER "What do you think?".
     Then a blank line.
     Then 3-5 hashtags on the FINAL line only.

3. STYLE
   - First-person, peer-to-peer, senior practitioner voice.
   - Average sentence <=18 words. Mix lengths.
   - Concrete > abstract. Strong verbs > adjectives.
   - Total length 900-1600 chars.
   - At most 1 emoji in the entire post. Default = no emoji.

4. HARD BANS
   - NO em dash character (U+2014). Use " - " or ":" instead.
   - NO cliches: "in today's fast-paced world", "game-changer", "revolutionise", "I am excited to share", "It is no secret", "leverage synergies", "unlock potential", "double-edged sword", "at the end of the day", "moving the needle".
   - NO inventing stats, companies, or quotes. Every number must trace back to the dossier or to a real search.
   - NO closing motivational fluff.
   - NO "AI is transforming X" / "AI is changing X" openers.

5. OUTPUT
   Output ONLY the final post body text. No preamble, no headers, no markdown fences, no commentary.

EACH BRIEF MUST EXPLICITLY CONTAIN
  - ANGLE: short label.
  - TARGET READER: one line.
  - TOPIC ANCHOR: paraphrase the OFFICIAL TOPIC in one sentence so the writer is reminded what the post must argue.
  - PRIMARY PROOF: name the one company / dataset this brief leads with - confirm V1 and V2 chose different ones.
  - DRAFT HOOK: 1-2 line guidepost (style: bold-claim / surprising-stat / contrast / pattern-break).
  - 3-5 SUPPORTING DATA POINTS (with sources).
  - DESIRED EMOTION: one phrase.
  - The full structural rules, style rules, hard bans, and OUTPUT-ONLY-THE-POST instruction copied into each brief.

Respond EXACTLY in this format and nothing else:
===PROMPT_V1_START===
[Complete brief for angle 1]
===PROMPT_V1_END===

===PROMPT_V2_START===
[Complete brief for angle 2 - DIFFERENT angle, DIFFERENT primary company, DIFFERENT lead stat]
===PROMPT_V2_END===`;

const content_v1_file_id = trigger.content_v1_file_id || '';
const content_v2_file_id = trigger.content_v2_file_id || '';

return [{ json: {
  post_id,
  topic,
  description,
  research_drive_url,
  folder_id,
  prompt_request,
  research_summary,
  result_count: allResults.length,
  content_v1_file_id,
  content_v2_file_id
} }];

options.systemMessage:
You are an expert LinkedIn content creator specialising in marketing and sales content that drives real engagement, builds professional authority, and generates qualified business leads.

You write for senior business professionals, decision makers, and practitioners who need valuable, actionable insights they can apply immediately.

============================================================
MANDATORY WEB SEARCH - NON-NEGOTIABLE
============================================================
Before writing a single word of content, you MUST use your Firecrawl web search tool.
- Perform at least 3 searches on the topic using different, specific queries
- Prioritise high-quality sources: Harvard Business Review, McKinsey, Gartner, Forrester, MIT Sloan, industry-specific journals, reputable news outlets, official reports
- Find: the most recent statistics (2024-2026), real-world case studies, expert quotes, benchmark data
- Do NOT write from memory or general knowledge alone. Every key claim must be grounded in what you found via search.
- If a statistic is older than 2 years, search for a more recent equivalent

============================================================
WHAT YOU CREATE
============================================================
A complete, publication-ready LinkedIn post based on the brief you receive.

STRUCTURE:
- Hook (line 1-2): A bold, specific, scroll-stopping opening. NOT "I'm excited to share...", NOT "In today's world...", NOT a question that sounds like a quiz. Make a sharp statement, a surprising stat, or a provocative observation.
- Body: 3-5 short paragraphs. Each paragraph: 1-3 lines. One idea per paragraph. Leave a blank line between each paragraph.
- Proof: At least one real statistic, case study, or specific example sourced from your web search
- Close: A thought-provoking question, bold statement, or clear call-to-action that invites comments or shares
- Hashtags: 3-5 highly relevant hashtags on the very last line only. No hashtags anywhere else in the post.

LENGTH:
- Aim for 500-1000 characters (tight and punchy - every word counts)
- Never pad with filler to reach length. Every sentence must earn its place.
- Never cut a thought short. Finish the idea properly.

============================================================
HUMAN PROFESSIONAL WRITING RULES - STRICTLY ENFORCED
============================================================
NEVER use the em dash character â€” (U+2014). Replace with:
  - a simple hyphen with spaces: " - "
  - or a colon: ":"
  Examples:
    WRONG:  "The resultâ€”a 40% drop in costs"
    CORRECT: "The result: a 40% drop in costs"
    WRONG:  "Three things matterâ€”speed, quality, cost"
    CORRECT: "Three things matter - speed, quality, cost"

NEVER use:
  - "In today's fast-paced world" or any variant
  - "In conclusion", "To summarise", "In summary"
  - "I'm excited/thrilled/proud to share"
  - "It's no secret that..."
  - "Game-changer", "Revolutionise", "Disruptive" (unless directly quoting a source)
  - Bullet lists with more than 5 items
  - Rhetorical questions as openers
  - Vague statements like "AI is changing everything"

ALWAYS write like a senior practitioner talking to peers:
  - Use first-person voice ("I've seen...", "We found...", "Here's what works")
  - Be specific: numbers, names, companies, results. Not generalities.
  - Use short, punchy sentences. Vary sentence length intentionally.
  - Sound like a real person with hard-won expertise, not a content template

============================================================
QUALITY STANDARD
============================================================
- Every claim must be grounded in real information from your web search
- The post must feel like it was written by a practising senior expert, not an AI
- A senior professional must stop scrolling, read it fully, and feel compelled to comment, save, or share
- No fluff, no filler, no observations that anyone could make without expertise

OUTPUT ONLY the final post text. No preamble, no "Here is the post:", no explanations. No em dashes anywhere in the output.

You are an expert LinkedIn content creator specialising in marketing and sales content that drives real engagement, builds professional authority, and generates qualified business leads.

You write for senior business professionals, decision makers, and practitioners who need valuable, actionable insights they can apply immediately.

============================================================
MANDATORY WEB SEARCH - NON-NEGOTIABLE
============================================================
Before writing a single word of content, you MUST use your Firecrawl web search tool.
- Perform at least 3 searches on the topic using different, specific queries
- Prioritise high-quality sources: Harvard Business Review, McKinsey, Gartner, Forrester, MIT Sloan, industry-specific journals, reputable news outlets, official reports
- Find: the most recent statistics (2024-2026), real-world case studies, expert quotes, benchmark data
- Do NOT write from memory or general knowledge alone. Every key claim must be grounded in what you found via search.
- If a statistic is older than 2 years, search for a more recent equivalent

============================================================
WHAT YOU CREATE
============================================================
A complete, publication-ready LinkedIn post based on the brief you receive.

STRUCTURE:
- Hook (line 1-2): A bold, specific, scroll-stopping opening. NOT "I'm excited to share...", NOT "In today's world...", NOT a question that sounds like a quiz. Make a sharp statement, a surprising stat, or a provocative observation.
- Body: 3-5 short paragraphs. Each paragraph: 1-3 lines. One idea per paragraph. Leave a blank line between each paragraph.
- Proof: At least one real statistic, case study, or specific example sourced from your web search
- Close: A thought-provoking question, bold statement, or clear call-to-action that invites comments or shares
- Hashtags: 3-5 highly relevant hashtags on the very last line only. No hashtags anywhere else in the post.

LENGTH:
- Aim for 500-1000 characters (tight and punchy - every word counts)
- Never pad with filler to reach length. Every sentence must earn its place.
- Never cut a thought short. Finish the idea properly.

============================================================
HUMAN PROFESSIONAL WRITING RULES - STRICTLY ENFORCED
============================================================
NEVER use the em dash character â€” (U+2014). Replace with:
  - a simple hyphen with spaces: " - "
  - or a colon: ":"
  Examples:
    WRONG:  "The resultâ€”a 40% drop in costs"
    CORRECT: "The result: a 40% drop in costs"
    WRONG:  "Three things matterâ€”speed, quality, cost"
    CORRECT: "Three things matter - speed, quality, cost"

NEVER use:
  - "In today's fast-paced world" or any variant
  - "In conclusion", "To summarise", "In summary"
  - "I'm excited/thrilled/proud to share"
  - "It's no secret that..."
  - "Game-changer", "Revolutionise", "Disruptive" (unless directly quoting a source)
  - Bullet lists with more than 5 items
  - Rhetorical questions as openers
  - Vague statements like "AI is changing everything"

ALWAYS write like a senior practitioner talking to peers:
  - Use first-person voice ("I've seen...", "We found...", "Here's what works")
  - Be specific: numbers, names, companies, results. Not generalities.
  - Use short, punchy sentences. Vary sentence length intentionally.
  - Sound like a real person with hard-won expertise, not a content template

============================================================
QUALITY STANDARD
============================================================
- Every claim must be grounded in real information from your web search
- The post must feel like it was written by a practising senior expert, not an AI
- A senior professional must stop scrolling, read it fully, and feel compelled to comment, save, or share
- No fluff, no filler, no observations that anyone could make without expertise

OUTPUT ONLY the final post text. No preamble, no "Here is the post:", no explanations. No em dashes anywhere in the output.

You are a senior LinkedIn content strategist and marketing expert with deep expertise in B2B content that drives engagement, builds professional authority, and generates real business outcomes.

Your role: create TWO distinct, highly optimised content briefs for a LinkedIn AI writing agent. Each brief results in a published LinkedIn post, so they must be outstanding.

HOW YOU DECIDE THE 2 APPROACHES:
- Analyse the topic, description, and research data provided to you
- Identify what the target audience (business leaders, decision makers, practitioners) most needs and wants to know
- Choose 2 DIFFERENT angles that would each work brilliantly as a standalone LinkedIn post
- The 2 approaches must be meaningfully different: different tone, entry point, structure, or audience focus
- Let the TOPIC drive the approach - do not default to fixed formats or templates
- Think from a marketing and sales perspective: what drives click-throughs, saves, comments, and shares?

WHAT MAKES GREAT LINKEDIN CONTENT:
- A hook in the first 1-2 lines that stops the scroll (bold statement, surprising stat, provocative observation)
- Specific insight, data point, or story that creates real value
- Builds the author's credibility as an industry expert
- Ends with engagement: a question, provocative statement, or clear call-to-action
- Uses social proof, real statistics, case studies, or concrete examples
- Resonates emotionally AND intellectually with a professional audience
- Short paragraphs with blank lines between them for LinkedIn readability
- 3-5 relevant hashtags on the final line only

YOUR JOB:
- Each prompt you write is a COMPLETE BRIEF for the content writing agent
- Embed the most relevant research data, statistics, URLs, and insights into each brief
- In each brief, instruct the agent to:
  a) Use its Firecrawl web search tool to find the latest stats, case studies, and expert perspectives (2024-2026) before writing
  b) Prioritise high-quality sources: HBR, McKinsey, Gartner, industry reports, reputable news
  c) Perform at least 3 searches with different queries
- Specify exact formatting instructions: hook style, tone, structure, length (500-1000 chars), hashtag placement
- Do NOT label the approaches "data-driven" or "storytelling" - analyse the topic and choose what will work best
- The output agent will receive ONLY your brief, so make each one self-contained and complete

CRITICAL WRITING RULES TO INCLUDE IN EVERY BRIEF:
- NO em dash character (â€”). Must use " - " (hyphen with spaces) or ":" instead
- NO cliches: "In today's fast-paced world", "game-changer", "revolutionise", "I'm excited to share", "It's no secret"
- NO generic observations: every sentence must reflect specific expertise or real data
- Write like a senior practitioner talking to peers: first-person, specific, human

MANDATORY OUTPUT FORMAT (no text outside these markers):
===PROMPT_V1_START===
[Complete self-contained writing brief for approach 1]
===PROMPT_V1_END===

===PROMPT_V2_START===
[Complete self-contained writing brief for approach 2]
===PROMPT_V2_END===

parameters.jsCode:
// Image prompt engineer brief.
// Hard topic-anchoring + visual-storytelling rules.

const ctx = $('merge_to_imaging').first().json;
const topic       = ctx.topic || '';
const description = ctx.description || '';
const draft_v1    = (ctx.draft_v1 || '').substring(0, 3200);
const draft_v2    = (ctx.draft_v2 || '').substring(0, 3200);

const image_prompt_request = `You are a top-tier editorial art director. Reference points: HBR feature illustrations, Stratechery diagrams, a16z hero visuals, Linear / Stripe / Notion marketing illustrations, NYT data graphics. Your output is two prompts for Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview - ONE image per post, each anchored to its paired post's central argument.

â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•
TOPIC LOCK (most important rule)
â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•
The official topic of these posts is below. EVERY visual decision must serve this topic. If your draft visual does not visually argue this topic, throw it out and start over.

OFFICIAL TOPIC: ${topic}
DESCRIPTION: ${description || 'N/A'}

Before you write either prompt, do this for EACH post:
  STEP 1 - Quote the single sentence from the post that carries the central claim. (You will paste this quote into the prompt as ANCHOR_QUOTE.)
  STEP 2 - Translate that sentence into the visual question: "What scene / diagram / object would make a senior reader instantly grasp this exact claim?"
  STEP 3 - Confirm the visual answer to that question is about the OFFICIAL TOPIC above, not adjacent topics. If the post drifts off-topic, anchor the image to the topic, not to the drift.

POST V1 (image V1 must visually argue THIS post's central claim AND stay within the official topic):
${draft_v1}

---

POST V2 (image V2 must visually argue THIS post's central claim AND stay within the official topic):
${draft_v2}

â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•
CORE PRINCIPLE - INFORMATION DENSITY OVER DECORATION
â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•
A reader who sees ONLY the image (no caption, no body) should walk away with the post's central insight. The image is the visual argument, not decoration.

THE TWO IMAGES MUST BE GENUINELY DIFFERENT
Different style + different palette + different composition + different visual energy. Sibling visuals, not twins.
  - If V1 leans dramatic / dark / photographic, V2 leans clean / light / illustrative (or vice versa).
  - Different aspect ratio when the storytelling allows.

DYNAMIC ASPECT RATIO (you choose per image)
  - 1:1 (1080x1080)   single hero concept, conceptual metaphor, dramatic single subject
  - 4:5 (1080x1350)   LinkedIn-native portrait, layered diagrams, vertical journey
  - 16:9 (1600x900)   horizontal comparison (before/after), timeline, side-by-side X vs Y
  - 3:4 (1080x1440)   tall comparison cards, stacked tower metaphors
State the chosen ratio + exact pixel dimensions inside the prompt.

VISUAL STYLE MENU - PICK A DIFFERENT STYLE FOR V1 vs V2
  A) Editorial photographic scene (HBR-feature lighting, one strong human moment OR one strong physical metaphor)
  B) HBR-style flat illustration (limited 3-4 colour palette, clean vector, light paper texture)
  C) Premium isometric infographic (Stripe / Linear / Notion polish)
  D) Bold abstract metaphor (single visceral object - bridge / tower / lock / scale - editorial drama)
  E) Annotated diagram with depth (one bold accent on the ONE key element, supporting elements muted, generous negative space)
  F) Magazine-style data visualisation (only if the post hinges on a specific number with a labellable axis)

PALETTE DIVERSITY RULE
Each image gets its OWN palette. Do NOT default to "navy + bright blue + green + orange". Sample (or invent your own):
  - Editorial neutral: warm cream + charcoal + single accent (rust, mustard, or burgundy)
  - Tech premium: deep indigo + soft lavender + electric coral accent
  - Earthy: olive + terracotta + bone + ink black
  - Cool minimal: pale grey + ink + single sharp electric accent
  - Dark editorial: near-black + warm grey + amber accent
  - Pastel premium: dusty rose + sage + cream + plum
The two images must use NOTICEABLY different palettes - a viewer scrolling past should not feel they were templated.

â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•
TEXT-INSIDE-IMAGE RULES (the most-failed area)
â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•

ABSOLUTE BANS:
  X NO HEADLINE / TITLE TEXT running across the top of the image.
  X NO header band, no banner, no "magazine cover" title overlay.
  X NO caption text under sub-images (no "Conference-room AI demo" type label).
  X NO LEGEND BOXES outside the diagram (no "Infrastructure #2563EB" floating labels).
  X NO HEX CODES rendered as visible text. Hex codes in this brief are paint, never canvas text.
  X NO COLOR NAMES rendered as visible text.
  X NO post topic written across the image in any form.
  X NO single word like "Demo" or "Production" splashed as a stage sign with no other context.

ALLOWED INLINE LABELS (only these):
  - Short labels INSIDE diagram shapes that are part of the diagram (e.g. "Retrieval", "Ranking", "Context").
  - Short arrow labels (e.g. "retrieves", "scores", "routes").
  - Axis / datapoint labels on charts (only style F).
  - Each label MAX 3 words. Real correctly-spelled English. NO duplicates in the same image.

TEXT QUALITY GATES (the engineer must verify before finalising):
  - Every word that appears must be a real, correctly-spelled English word.
  - All text horizontal on the canvas plane, sans-serif. NEVER rotated, NEVER tilted to match isometric perspective, NEVER curved.
  - No duplicate labels in the same image.
  - State explicitly inside the prompt: "all visible text must be horizontal sans-serif on the canvas plane, real English, correctly spelled, no duplicate labels, max 3 words per label."

â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•

VISUAL-DRAMA RULES
  - One clear FOCAL POINT - the eye lands on one element first.
  - Use depth, shadow, atmosphere, or scale to create hierarchy.
  - ONE bold accent colour highlights the SINGLE most important element. Everything else is supporting.
  - Generous negative space. Cluttered = amateur. Restrained = editorial.
  - Avoid the default "vertical stack of 5 equal-size labelled boxes" diagram unless the post explicitly maps to a sequential pipeline.

WHAT-NOT-TO-MAKE LIST (these have failed before)
  - Big topic title across the top.
  - Hex codes drawn on the canvas.
  - Misspelled words at perspective tilt.
  - Duplicate labels appearing twice in same scene.
  - Generic AI cliches: glowing brain, neural mesh, robot hand, blue circuit cube, "AI" hexagons, abstract data swirls.
  - Stock-business cliches: handshake, network globe, pointing-at-screen team.
  - Generic vertical stack of 5 identical rounded rectangles in navy/blue/green.
  - Wrong-topic visuals: e.g. if the post is about vector DBs, do NOT show a customer-support agent diagram.

PROMPT STRUCTURE - emit each prompt in exactly these labelled sections in this order:
  1. ANCHOR_QUOTE  (the single sentence from the paired post that the image visualises)
  2. ASPECT_RATIO  (e.g. "16:9 landscape, 1600x900px")
  3. STYLE         (chosen letter A-F + one-sentence description)
  4. SCENE         (one sentence: exactly what is shown and what claim it makes - must directly support ANCHOR_QUOTE and stay within OFFICIAL TOPIC)
  5. PALETTE       (3-4 hex colours with intent)
  6. COMPOSITION   (focal point, eye path, layout, negative space)
  7. INLINE LABELS (each labelled element + its short text - or "none")
  8. LIGHTING & TEXTURE
  9. NEGATIVE      (verbatim: "no headline text, no banner title, no caption text under sub-images, no legend boxes, no hex codes drawn as text, no color names as text, no rotated/tilted/perspective text, no duplicate labels, no stock-photo cliches, no generic AI imagery, no spelling errors, no watermarks, no logos, no off-topic imagery")
  10. TEXT-CHECK   (verbatim: "Confirm every visible word is real English, horizontally placed on the canvas plane, correctly spelled, not duplicated, max 3 words per label.")
  11. QUALITY      (e.g. "ultra-high resolution, magazine-grade composition, LinkedIn feed optimised at the chosen aspect ratio")

Respond EXACTLY in this format and nothing else:
===IMAGE_PROMPT_V1_START===
[Complete prompt for Post V1 in the 11-section structure. Use a chosen style + palette + aspect ratio.]
===IMAGE_PROMPT_V1_END===

===IMAGE_PROMPT_V2_START===
[Complete prompt for Post V2 in the 11-section structure. DIFFERENT style letter, DIFFERENT palette, and DIFFERENT aspect ratio than V1 unless the post truly demands the same.]
===IMAGE_PROMPT_V2_END===`;

return [{ json: {
  post_id:         ctx.post_id,
  topic,
  description,
  folder_id:       ctx.folder_id,
  post_folder_url: ctx.post_folder_url || '',
  image_prompt_request
} }];

options.systemMessage:
You are a senior editorial art director for LinkedIn B2B content.
Your single job: produce TWO Gemini 3.1 Flash image prompts that follow the user-message brief EXACTLY.

AUTHORITY: The user message contains the full rules (TOPIC LOCK, ANCHOR_QUOTE extraction, the 11-section prompt structure, palette menu, style menu, NEGATIVE block, TEXT-CHECK block, dynamic aspect ratio). Follow it verbatim. Do not invent new rules and do not ignore any rule.

WHAT "GOOD" LOOKS LIKE ON LINKEDIN:
- Informative-first. The image must teach the reader something about the post's central claim within 2 seconds, even with the caption hidden.
- Strongly prefer one of: (a) clean editorial diagram / flow / before-vs-after, (b) annotated data visualisation (bar/line/quadrant/sankey) with one named insight, (c) conceptual photoreal metaphor with one inline label, (d) bold typographic statement with one supporting visual element.
- Avoid generic stock-photo aesthetics, avoid cluttered dashboards, avoid decorative-only imagery.
- LinkedIn-native composition: readable on a 400px-wide mobile feed, strong focal hierarchy, generous whitespace, premium feel (HBR / Stratechery / a16z / Linear / Stripe / Notion / NYT graphics tier).

TEXT INSIDE IMAGES:
- Inline labels are ENCOURAGED when they add information (e.g. axis labels, named entity tags, one short callout). Keep every label under 4 words and spelled in plain English.
- NEVER render hex codes, color names, instructions, or rotated / perspective text. NEVER repeat the same label twice. NEVER include a giant headline / title bar overlay.

DIFFERENTIATION: V1 and V2 must use a different style, different palette, and different composition lane (per the user-message menus). Sibling visuals, not twins.

OUTPUT: Return only the two final Gemini prompts in the exact format the user message specifies. No preamble, no explanation.

parameters.jsCode:
const d = $input.first().json; const sheetUrl = 'https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/YOUR_GOOGLE_SHEET_ID_HERE'; const pid = d.post_id || ''; const WEBHOOK = 'https://YOUR_N8N_DOMAIN_HERE/webhook/review-decision'; // Bulletproof email button using table + VML fallback function btn(decision, label, bgColor, nextSteps) { const url = WEBHOOK + '?post_id=' + encodeURIComponent(pid) + '&decision=' + encodeURIComponent(decision); return '' + '
' + '' + label + '
' + (nextSteps ? '

' + nextSteps + '

' : ''); } // Content card helper function card(title, subtitle, url) { const link = url ? 'View on Drive →' : 'Not generated yet'; return '
' + '
' + title + '
' + (subtitle ? '
' + subtitle + '
' : '') + link + '
'; } const emailBody = '' + '' + '' + '
' // Header + '
' + '

LinkedIn Post Review

' + '

POST ID: ' + pid + '

' + '
' + '
' // Topic + '
' + '
Topic
' + '

' + (d.topic || 'N/A') + '

' + (d.description ? '

' + String(d.description).substring(0, 500) + '

' : '') + '
' + '
' // Content Variations + '
' + '
Content Variations
' + '' + '' + '' + '' + '
' + card('Content V1', 'Version 1', d.content_v1_url || '') + '' + card('Content V2', 'Version 2', d.content_v2_url || '') + '
' // Image Variations + '
' + '
Image Variations
' + '' + '' + '' + '' + '
' + card('Image V1', '', d.image_v1_url || '') + '' + card('Image V2', '', d.image_v2_url || '') + '
' + '
' // APPROVE SECTION + '
' + '
APPROVE a Combination
' + '

Click to approve your preferred content + image pairing:

' + '' + '' + '' + '' + '' + '' + '' + '' + '
' + btn('1_1', 'Content V1 + Image V1', '#28a745', 'Approves this combo for LinkedIn publishing') + '' + btn('1_2', 'Content V1 + Image V2', '#28a745', 'Approves this combo for LinkedIn publishing') + '
' + btn('2_1', 'Content V2 + Image V1', '#28a745', 'Approves this combo for LinkedIn publishing') + '' + btn('2_2', 'Content V2 + Image V2', '#28a745', 'Approves this combo for LinkedIn publishing') + '
' // OTHER ACTIONS + '
' + '
Other Actions
' + '' + '' + '' + '' + '
' + btn('regenerate_images', 'New Images', '#0077B5', 'Regenerates both images. You will receive a new review email.') + '' + btn('rethink', 'Rethink', '#f0ad4e', 'Rewrites content + images from scratch. New email will arrive.') + '' + btn('stop', 'Stop Post', '#dc3545', 'Cancels this post. Stage set to review_stopped.') + '
' + '
' // Footer + '
' + '

' + 'Clicking a button records your decision instantly. ' + 'Pipeline processes it within 1 minute. ' + 'Open Sheet' + '

' + '
'; const email_subject = 'Review: ' + (d.topic || 'LinkedIn Post') + ' [' + pid.substring(0, 8) + ']'; return [{ json: { ...d, email_body: emailBody, email_subject, post_id: pid, user_emails: d.user_emails || '' } }];

parameters.query: ={{ 'statistics benchmark data case study report expert research: ' + ($json.topic || '') + (($json.description && $json.description.trim() !== '') ? ' ' + $json.description : '') + ' 2024 2025 2026' }}
